examples: If Catholics were only faithful to the teachings of the Magisterium, he wrote, all would be well.
Personal conscience and reason should not be set in opposition to the moral law or the Magisterium of the Church.
The church he loved was celibate, hostile to women priests and set against gays; if Catholics were only faithful to the teachings of the Magisterium, he wrote, all would be well.
Muddling through is no substitute for clarity and the church is now paying for its fatuity and lack of a magisterium.
In the book, the Magisterium, this awful group is constantly being referred to as the church, but you're in another ear and alternate world.
